lt.gd: This bill supplements or reduces appropriations otherwise provided for the support and operation of state government for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025 and ending June 30, 2026 and for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026 and ending June 30, 2027.
lt.hp: This bill:provides budget increases and decreases for the use and support of certain state agencies;provides budget increases and decreases for the use and support of certain public education programs;provides budget increases and decreases for the use and support of certain institutions of higher education;provides funds for the bills with fiscal impact passed in the 2026 General Session;provides budget increases and decreases for other purposes as described; andprovides intent language.
lt.moni: Money Appropriated in this Bill:This bill appropriates $236,345,400 in operating and capital budgets for fiscal year 2026, including:$4,311,500 from General Fund; and($3,208,800) from Income Tax Fund; and$235,242,700 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $83,300 in expendable funds and accounts for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $58,400 in business-like activities for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates ($11,250,000) in restricted fund and account transfers for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $3,000,000 in transfers to unrestricted funds for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $565,849,700 in operating and capital budgets for fiscal year 2027, including:$38,684,300 from General Fund; and$25,344,900 from Income Tax Fund; and$23,305,300 from Uniform School Fund; and$478,515,200 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $66,500 in expendable funds and accounts for fiscal year 2027, including:($500,000) from General Fund; and$566,500 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $743,000 in business-like activities for fiscal year 2027, including:$200,000 from General Fund; and$543,000 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates ($28,731,800) in restricted fund and account transfers for fiscal year 2027, including:($12,081,800) from General Fund; and($16,650,000) from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates ($1,400,000) in capital project funds for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill reflects $3,200,000 in higher education budget reporting for fiscal year 2027.
lt.oc: Other Special Clauses:This bill provides a special effective date.

leg.enact: Be it enacted by the Legislature of the state of Utah:

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025, and ending June 30, 2026. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2026.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

lineitem.name: Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice
lineitem.appintent: To implement the provisions of Criminal and Juvenile Justice Changes (House Bill 48, 2026 General Session)

lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice
lineitem.appintent: To implement the provisions of Judicial Performance Evaluation Amendments (Senate Bill 233, 2026 General Session)

lineitem.name: Board of Pardons and Parole Operations
lineitem.lidesc: Board of Pardons and Parole - Board of Pardons and Parole Operations
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $1,200,000 provided for the Board of Pardons and Parole - Board of Pardons and Parole in Item 45, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $500,000 for O-Track Expansion Anticipated Tech Upgrades; up to $150,000 for Phase Three: BOPPConnect (Information Sharing with Courts); up to $400,000 for Phase Three: MyVoice (Victim Support); up to $100,000 for Pro-Tempore Board Members; and up to $50,000 for contract costs associated with defense counsel for offenders or offender evaluations.

lineitem.name: Contracts and Leases
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Contracts and Leases
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that $1,000,000 provided for the Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Contracts and Leases in Item 53, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $1,000,000 for one-time facility-related expenses.

lineitem.name: Driver License
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Driver License
lineitem.appintent: Notwithstanding intent language included in Senate Bill 3 "Appropriations Adjustments" (2025 General Session), item 160, the Legislature intends that the Department of Public Safety - Driver License Division provide a direct award grant of $500,000 to GET Group North America in fiscal year 2026 to support improvements in age verification technology and infrastructure in Utah. Funds may be used to support the development, deployment, and adoption of verification technologies used by authorized establishments, including bars and state liquor stores, to verify age when individuals present a state digital identity credential.

lineitem.name: Pass-Through
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity - Pass-Through
lineitem.appintent: Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity provide a direct award grant of $250,000 to Utah Tech Week in fiscal year 2026 to support a statewide technology ecosystem activation initiative that convenes entrepreneurs, startups, investors, workforce participants, and employers through coordinated events across Utah.

lineitem.name: Division of Housing and Community Development
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity - Division of Housing and Community Development
lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that, if "Housing and Community Development Amendments" (House Bill 68, 2026 General Session) passes and becomes law, the Division of Finance, when closing fiscal year 2026, transfer nonlapsing balances that relate to the consolidation of the state's efforts on housing, as a result of the bill, from the Department of Workforce Services to the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity, as agreed upon by the Department of Workforce Services.

agency.name: Department of Cultural and Community Engagement
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF CULTURAL AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT

lineitem.name: Arts & Museums Grants
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Cultural and Community Engagement - Arts Museums Grants
lineitem.appintent: Under Section 63J-1-603 of the Utah Code, the Legislature intends that the appropriation of General Fund provided by Item 78, Chapter 2, Laws of Utah 2025 for the Department of Cultural and Community Engagement - Arts and Museums Grants line item not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of these nonlapsing funds is limited to: contractual obligations and support for one-time appropriations to include Hope of Utah $125,000, Latinos in Action $250,000, Sevier County Archives and Heritage Space $50,000, Syracuse Museum Foundation $50,000, and Youth Art Access $100,000.

lineitem.name: Capital Facilities Grants
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Cultural and Community Engagement - Capital Facilities Grants
lineitem.appintent: Notwithstanding intent language in Item 18 of "Economic and Community Development Base Budget" (Senate Bill 4, 2026 General Session) and under Section 63J-1-603 of Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that appropriation of $4,125,000 General Fund provided by Item 79, Chapter 2, Laws of Utah 2025 for the Department of Cultural and Community Engagement - Capital Facilities Grants line item not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of these nonlapsing funds is limited to: contractual obligations and support for one-time appropriations to include Holy Trinity Cathedral $1,000,000, Salt Lake City Cemetery $3,000,000, and Ballet West $125,000.

lineitem.name: Heritage & Events Grants
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Cultural and Community Engagement - Heritage Events Grants
lineitem.appintent:
Notwithstanding intent language in Item 19 of "Economic and Community Development Base Budget" (Senate Bill 4, 2026 General Session) and under Section 63J-1-603 of Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that appropriation of $1,650,000 General Fund provided by Item 79, Chapter 2, Laws of Utah 2025 for the Department of Cultural and Community Engagement - Heritage and Events Grants line item not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of these nonlapsing funds is limited to: contractual obligations and support for one-time appropriations to include America's Freedom Festival at Provo $100,000, Days of 47 Rodeo $200,000, Ogden Pioneer Days Miss Rodeo/Cowboy Hall of Fame $150,000, Warriors Over the Wasatch/Hill AFB Show $200,000, Utah Symphony/Utah Opera AM250 Tour $600,000, and First Lady Foundation $400,000.
Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $1,000,000 of General Fund appropriations provided for the Department of Cultural and Community Engagement - Heritage and Events Grants for the Great American State Fair not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Us of any of the funds is limited to: up to $1,000,000 for the Great American State Fair.

lineitem.name: Operations and Policy
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Workforce Services - Operations and Policy
lineitem.appintent: Notwithstanding intent language in Item 38 of "Current Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations" (Senate Bill 3, 2026 General Session), under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $550,000 provided for the Department of Workforce Services - Operations and Policy in Item 86, Chapter 2, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $550,000 for SUN Bucks or Summer EBT program during fiscal year 2027.

Offering Registration (SCOR): Variable; and 4) Rules and Form Booklet: Actual Cost.

lineitem.appintent:
The Legislature intends that the Division of Finance, when closing fiscal year 2026, transfer any balance in the Utah Community Center for the Deaf Fund to the Schools for the Deaf and Blind Donation Fund.
The Legislature intends that, if House Bill 545 passes and becomes law, the Division of Finance, when closing fiscal year 2026, transfer any balances in the Wildlife Resources Conservation Easement Restricted Account and the Wild Game Meat Donation Fund to the Wildlife Resources Restricted Account.
The Legislature intends that the Division of Finance, when closing fiscal year 2026, transfer any balance in the repealed Office of Rail Safety Account to the Rail Transportation Subaccount.

lineitem.name: Education and General
lineitem.lidesc: University of Utah - Education and General

lineitem.name: Education and General
lineitem.lidesc: Utah State University - Education and General
lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that Utah State University use $1,500,000 one-time funding provided by this item to fund a cross-departmental project to incorporate an odometry lab and a proof of concept for communications-based train control on a limited portion of a light rail vehicle line. The goal of this project is to determine the best and most appropriate signaling system for current and future transit operations in the Salt Lake Valley, and to promote a center of excellence at Utah State University for such systems.

lineitem.name: Education and General
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Valley University - Education and General

lineitem.name: Education and General
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Valley University - Education and General
lineitem.appintent:
To implement the provisions of Elections Study (House Bill 311, 2026 General Session)
The Legislature intends that $100,000 of this item be used by The Herbert Institute for Public Policy and The Center for Constitutional Studies at Utah Valley University to conduct an elections study regarding the security of in-person voting versus voting by mail and the best practices for conducting and implementing a process where identification must be presented before depositing a ballot in a ballot drop box. The Legislature further intends that the institute and the center present the study to the Government Operations Interim Committee.

appsrc.name: Long-term Capital Projects Fund
appsrc.display: From Long-term Capital Projects Fund, One-time

lineitem.appintent: Notwithstanding language included in H.B. 5, "Natural Resources, Agriculture, and Environmental Quality Base Budget" (2026 General Session), item 41, under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $30,000,000 appropriated from the Long-term Capital Projects Fund to the Department of Natural Resources - Forestry, Fire, and State Lands not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of the nonlapsing funds is limited to $30,000,000 for property and/or water rights acquisition for the benefit of the Great Salt Lake. The Legislature further intends that any private contributions received for an acquisition financed with this appropriation be repaid to the Long-term Capital Projects Fund.

lineitem.name: Oil, Gas, and Mining
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Natural Resources - Oil, Gas, and Mining
lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that the Division of Oil, Gas, and Mining utilize up to $200,000 of the General Fund appropriation for Great Salt Lake Aquifer Restoration to determine if the project is economically viable and report on their findings to the Natural Resources, Agriculture, and Environmental Quality Appropriations Subcommittee by October 1, 2026, before expending the remaining appropriation. Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code, the Legislature intends that this one-time General Fund not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026.

lineitem.name: Department Oversight
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Department Oversight
lineitem.appintent:
Pursuant to Section 63J-1-603 of the Utah Code, the Legislature intends that under Item 38 of Chapter 9, Laws of Utah 2024 up to $505,000 General Fund provided for the Department of Health and Human Services Department Oversight line item shall not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. The use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to (1) $210,000 to health facility plan review activities in Health Facility Licensing and Certification, (2) $150,000 to health facility licensure and certification activities in Health Facility Licensing and Certification, and (3) $145,000 to Office of Background Processing for replacement of live scan machines, and enhancements and maintenance of the Direct Access Clearing System.
Pursuant to Section 63J-1-603 of the Utah Code, the Legislature intends that under Item 38 of Chapter 9, Laws of Utah 2025 up to $150,000 General Fund provided for the Department of Health and Human Services Department Oversight line item shall not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. The use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to expenditures for data processing and technology-based expenditures; facility repairs, maintenance, and improvements; and short-term projects and studies that promote efficiency and service improvement.

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2027. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2027.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

lineitem.name: Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice

program.display: Utah Office for Victims of Crime

lineitem.appintent:
The Legislature intends that the Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice use the $200,000 one-time appropriated in this item for "Felony Probation Pilot Program" in Cache County and that the Commission report by the October 2026 and 2027 interims on results from the grant recipient, including information related to recidivism, successful program completion, and re-integration.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice provide a direct award grant of $150,000 to Friends of the Children-Utah in Fiscal Year 2027 for a long-term professional mentoring youth program.

lineitem.name: Economic Prosperity
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity - Economic Prosperity
lineitem.appintent: Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity use funds appropriated in Item 25 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations" (H.B. 2, 2026 General Session) to: provide a $600,000 ongoing direct award grant to a statewide nonprofit organization representing Utah's aerospace and defense industry cluster to support the deployment of operational Advanced Air Mobility capabilities in preparation for the 2034 Olympic and Paralympic Winter Games and to position Utah as a national leader in next-generation aviation; and provide a $650,000 one-time direct award grant to the same organization to support Utah's effort to secure and operate a Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) OnRamp Hub, expanding defense industry partnerships, accelerating Department of Defense adoption of Utah-based technologies, and leveraging federal matching funds.

lineitem.appintent: To implement the provisions of State Permitting Council (House Bill 530, 2026 General Session)

lineitem.name: Pass-Through
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity - Pass-Through
lineitem.appintent:
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity provide an additional direct award grant of $100,000 to the Utah Industry Resource Alliance in fiscal year 2027 to assist Utah manufacturers and rural Utah businesses to improve operational excellence, accelerate revenue, and develop leadership.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity provide a direct award grant of $750,000 to Afforda in fiscal year 2027 to support their revolving loan program that targets the early-stage financing gap for affordable multifamily housing development.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity provide a direct award grant of $200,000 to Solutions Utah in fiscal year 2027 to support statewide education opportunities in policy leadership, research, civic engagement capacity addressing complex public challenges such as homelessness, public safety, and system accountability.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Development provide an additional direct award grant of $200,000 to the Five County Association of Governments in fiscal year 2027 for the Rural Business and Agricultural E-Commerce Accelerator to provide technical assistance and training to build e-commerce websites, diversify revenue, and increase online sales.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity provide a direct award grant of $100,000 to Utah Global Diplomacy in fiscal year 2027 for programmatic support, hosting opportunities, and personnel costs.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity provide a direct award grant of $1,500,000 to Utah's Habitat for Humanity in fiscal year 2027 for land acquisition, infrastructure, building materials, and project delivery on affordable housing projects.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity provide a direct award grant of $450,000 to Taste Utah in fiscal year 2027 for an advertisement campaign, and promotion of Utah restaurants and the Taste Utah Restaurant Marketing Campaign.

lineitem.name: Arts & Museums Grants
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Cultural and Community Engagement - Arts Museums Grants
lineitem.appintent:
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Utah Department of Cultural and Community Engagement provide a direct award grant of $200,000 to the Aerospace Heritage Foundation of Utah in fiscal year 2027 in support of the Hill Aerospace Museum Gateway project, and renovating and expanding the Hill Aerospace Museum lobby and Hadley Gallery entrance.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Cultural and Community Engagement provide a direct award grant of $60,000 to Tracy Aviary in fiscal year 2027 for operational support, capital projects, and programmatic support.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Cultural and Community Engagement provide a direct award grant of $75,000 to Sevier County Archives and Heritage Services in fiscal year 2027 for the establishment of an archiving management system, training and coordination of the archivist and historians, and development of a searchable county history website.

agency.name: Department of Veterans and Military Affairs
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AND MILITARY AFFAIRS

lineitem.name: DVMA Pass Through
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Veterans and Military Affairs - DVMA Pass Through
lineitem.appintent:
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use the one-time appropriations in this item to provide a direct award grant of $100,000 to Blue Star Families for military and veteran family programs. In its grant contract, the department shall ensure that grant funds are used only to provide programs and services that benefit service members, veterans, and their families who are Utah residents or are stationed in Utah.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use ongoing appropriations in Item 21 of "National Guard, Veterans Affairs, and Legislature Base Budget" (Senate Bill 7, 2026 General Session) to provide a direct award grant of $250,000 to Best Defense Foundation in fiscal year 2027 for veteran rehabilitation and wellness. In its grant contract, the department shall ensure that grant funds are used only to provide programs and services that benefit veterans who are Utah residents, or veteran events held in Utah. Funding provided by this item shall only be paid on a reimbursement basis; all requested documentation related to reimbursement shall be free of redaction.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use ongoing appropriations in Item 21 of "National Guard, Veterans Affairs, and Legislature Base Budget" (Senate Bill 7, 2026 General Session) to provide a direct award grant of $500,000 to the Veterans First Time Homebuyer Grant Program in fiscal year 2027 for first time homebuyer grants.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use ongoing appropriations in Item 21 of "National Guard, Veterans Affairs, and Legislature Base Budget" (Senate Bill 7, 2026 General Session) to provide a direct award grant of $900,000 to Counselors for Military School Children in fiscal year 2027 for increased counseling for children of military families.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use ongoing appropriations in Item 21 of "National Guard, Veterans Affairs, and Legislature Base Budget" (Senate Bill 7, 2026 General Session) to provide a direct award grant of $200,000 to National Ability Center in fiscal year 2027 for rehabilitation programs for veterans.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use ongoing appropriations in Item 21 of "National Guard, Veterans Affairs, and Legislature Base Budget" (Senate Bill 7, 2026 General Session) to provide a direct award grant of $60,000 to New Smiles for Veterans in fiscal year 2027 for veteran dental care.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use ongoing appropriations in Item 21 of "National Guard, Veterans Affairs, and Legislature Base Budget" (Senate Bill 7, 2026 General Session) to provide a direct award grant of $12,500 to Honor Flight in fiscal year 2027 for transportation of veterans to national landmarks.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use ongoing appropriations in Item 21 of "National Guard, Veterans Affairs, and Legislature Base Budget" (Senate Bill 7, 2026 General Session) to provide a direct award grant of $10,000 to Western Regional Partnership in fiscal year 2027 for annual dues.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Veterans and Military Affairs use one-time appropriations in this item to provide a direct award grant of $40,000 to Utah Golf Foundation for the Veterans on Course program.

lineitem.name: Chief Information Officer
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Government Operations - Chief Information Officer
lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that $1.5 million one-time for Utah Cyber Center - Local Government Cybersecurity Support be used to establish a matching grant program for local government entities and special service districts to strengthen cybersecurity at public water and wastewater systems. Grants awarded under this program shall require a local match and be used for cybersecurity assessments, planning, technology acquisition, and related implementation activities that enhance the resiliency of critical water infrastructure.

lineitem.appintent:
To implement the provisions of Outcome-based Investment Grant Pilot Framework (Senate Bill 324, 2026 General Session)
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63N Chapter 6, the Legislature intends that the State Treasurer transfer $1,500,000 from the Fund of Funds to the Utah Capital Investment Restricted Account for the purpose of Higher Education administering the Outcome-based Investment Grant Pilot Framework described in Section 63G-6b-402.

lineitem.name: Education and General
lineitem.lidesc: Utah State University - Education and General
lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that Utah State University use $500,000 ongoing funding provided by this item to fund a cross-departmental project to incorporate an odometry lab and a proof of concept for communications-based train control on a limited portion of a light rail vehicle line. The goal of this project is to determine the best and most appropriate signaling system for current and future transit operations in the Salt Lake Valley, and to promote a center of excellence at Utah State University for such systems.

The Legislature intends that the Department of Environmental Quality Division of Water Quality charge $135 per hour for the following fees: 1) Groundwater Permits and Permits by Rule; 2) Construction Permits; and 3) Underground Injection Control Permit Renewal and Modifications.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the one-time General Fund appropriation provided in Item 128 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations Act" (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session) be used by the Department of Environmental Quality to provide a competitive grant for Mantua Reservoir Restoration.

lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that, if "Hunting and Fishing Amendments" (House Bill 168, 2026 General Session) or "Wildlife Amendments" (House Bill 111, 2026 General Session) passes and becomes law, beginning July 1, 2026, the following fees shall become effective: Nonresident Guide Registration, All Species Big game or Bear: $750.00; Nonresident Guide Registration, Small Game and Fish: $500.00; Nonresident Outfitter Registration, All Species Big Game or Bear: $1,500.00; Nonresident Outfitter Registration, Small Game Upland and Fish: $1,000.00; Resident Guide Registration, All Species Big Game or Bear: $250.00; Resident Guide Registration, Small Game and Fish: $175.00; Resident Outfitter Registration, All Species Big Game or Bear: $500.00; and Resident Outfitter Registration, Small Game Upland and Fish: $350.00.

lineitem.name: Utah Schools for the Deaf and the Blind
lineitem.lidesc: State Board of Education - Utah Schools for the Deaf and the Blind
lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that the State Board of Education study the adoption of a new salary schedule for licensed educators at the Utah Schools for the Deaf and the Blind to include a more linear structure, like those used by many local education agencies, eliminating the use of traditional steps and lanes, incorporating measures of educator performance and effectiveness, and calculating annual cost of living increases into the compensation plan. The Legislature further intends that the State Board of Education report findings and recommendations to the Public Education Appropriations Subcommittee by October 15, 2026.

program.name: Non-Medicaid Behavioral Health Treatment and Crisis Response
program.display: Non-Medicaid Behavioral Health Treatment and Crisis Response

lineitem.appintent:
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide a direct award grant of $50,000 one-time in fiscal year 2027 to Flourish Therapy, Inc. for the provision of mental health services.
The Legislature intends that the funding item called OUD Treatment at Community Health Centers, funded during the 2026 General Session shall be included in the Fiscal Note and Budget Item Follow-Up report to the Executive Appropriations Committee required under 63J-1-903.
The Legislature intends that the funding item called Expanding Prevention, Treatment and Recovery Services for OUD, funded during the 2026 General Session shall be included in the Fiscal Note and Budget Item Follow-Up report to the Executive Appropriations Committee required under 63J-1-903.
The Legislature intends that the funding item called Opioid Prevention Shifting Efforts Upstream, funded during the 2026 General Session shall be included in the Fiscal Note and Budget Item Follow-Up report to the Executive Appropriations Committee required under 63J-1-903.
Notwithstanding the intent language included in Item 158 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations" (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session), which states, "Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide a direct award grant of $20,000 to the Men's Mental Health and Suicide Prevention Initiative in Fiscal Year 2027 for community-based, preventative mental health services for men.", under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide a direct award grant of $20,000 to the I Love You Bro Project in fiscal year 2027 from Item 158 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations" (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session), for a men's mental health and suicide prevention initiative.
The Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services, the Association for Utah Community Health, or other recipients of funding via Opioid Use Disorder Treatment at Community Health Centers report to the Social Services Appropriations Subcommittee by January 1, 2029 on (1) outcomes achieved, (2) advisability of continuing funding, and (3) challenges faced in reaching desired outcomes.
The Legislature intends that the following intent language included in Item 158 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations" (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session), which states, "Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide direct award grants of $150,000 to Fit to Recover and $100,000 to the School of Addiction Recovery in fiscal year 2027 for services to individuals recovering from substance use disorders" is rescinded.
Notwithstanding the intent language included in Item 158 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations" (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session), which states, "Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide a direct award grant of $269,900 to Jacky's Recovery Support Services in fiscal year 2027 to increase the number of rural and frontier Utahns receiving prevention, treatment and recovery services for opiate use disorder.", under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide a direct award grant of $100,000 to Jacky's Recovery Support Services in fiscal year 2027 from Item 158 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations" (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session), to increase the number of rural and frontier Utahns receiving prevention, treatment and recovery services for opiate use disorder.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide a direct award grant of $250,000 ongoing and $169,600 one-time in fiscal year 2027 Opioid Litigation Proceeds Funds from New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session) to the Association for Utah Community Health Centers to provide treatment and recovery services for opiate use disorder.

lineitem.appintent:
Notwithstanding intent language in Item 171 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations" (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session), under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Division of Facilities Construction and Management provide a direct award grant of up to $1,500,000 one-time from the General Fund to the Aspen Center for Learning for the Aspen Center for Learning Project in fiscal year 2027.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Division of Facilities Construction and Management provide a direct award grant of up to $500,000 one-time from the General Fund in fiscal year 2027 to the Refuge Utah to construct a new victim services campus in Utah County.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Division of Facilities Construction and Management provide a direct award grant of up to $100,000 one-time in fiscal year 2027 from the General Fund to the Fellowship Foundation of Utah for structural improvements to the facility owned and operated by the Fellowship Foundation of Utah.

lineitem.name: Pass-Through
lineitem.lidesc: Transportation - Pass-Through
lineitem.appintent:
Notwithstanding the intent language included in the introduced version of H.B. 2, Item 179, lines 2841-2848, and in accordance with Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Transportation provide a direct grant of up to $250,000 one-time from the General Fund in fiscal year 2027 to the Central Wasatch Commission for canyon transportation and graffiti remediation.
The Legislature intends that the Pioneer Pipeline expansion project from Wyoming to West Bountiful move forward expeditiously to increase Utah's gasoline supply and improve the state's fuel supply reliability. The Legislature encourages affected political subdivisions to work collaboratively with pipeline project sponsors and state agencies to ensure that permitting and regulatory processes are conducted in a coordinated, transparent, and timely manner, with the goal of achieving an additional 1.4 million gallons per day of transportation fuels by 2028.

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following expendable funds. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated. Outlays and expenditures from the funds or accounts to which the money is transferred may be made without further legislative action, in accordance with statutory provisions relating to the funds or accounts.

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following proprietary funds. Under the terms and conditions of Utah Code 63J-1-410, for any included Internal Service Fund, the Legislature approves budgets, full-time permanent positions, and capital acquisition amounts as indicated, and appropriates to the funds, as indicated, estimated revenue from rates, fees, and other charges. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated.

lineitem.name: Water Infrastructure Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Natural Resources - Water Infrastructure Fund
lineitem.appintent:
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the one-time General Fund appropriation provided in Item 224 of "New Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations Act" (House Bill 2, 2026 General Session) be used by the Water Development Coordinating Council to provide the following direct award grants: $4.0 million for Effluent Water Reuse Project - Phase 2 and $500,000 for Cache Valley/North Logan Fire Prevention and Water System Improvements.
Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the one-time General Fund appropriation provided in this item be used by the Department of Natural Resources to provide a competitive grant for addressing sedimentation in Pine Valley Reservoir.

subsection.amend: Except as provided in Subsection (2), this bill takes effect:
except as provided in Subsection (1)(b), May 6, 2026; or
if approved by two-thirds of all members elected to each house:
without the governor's signature, the day following the constitutional time limit of Utah Constitution, Article VII, Section 8; or
in the case of a veto, the date of veto override.
The actions affecting Section 2 (Effective 07/01/26) take effect on July 1, 2026.
